name: Startup India DPIIT Recognition
implementing_agency: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade
description: Official government recognition for startups enabling income tax exemptions for 3 years, self-certification under 9 labour and 3 environment laws, fast-track patent examination at 80% fee rebate, and access to Fund of Funds.
benefits: Income tax exemption for 3 consecutive years out of first 10 years
Self-certification under 9 labour laws and 3 environment laws
Access to Fund of Funds via SIDBI

name: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS)
description: Provides financial assistance to startups for proof of concept, prototype development, product trials, market entry, and commercialisation through DPIIT-approved incubators. Grants up to ₹20 lakh and loans up to ₹50 lakh.
benefits: Grant up to ₹20 lakh for validation and prototype
Debt/convertible debenture up to ₹50 lakh for commercialisation

name: Fund of Funds for Startups (FFS)
description: ₹10,000 crore corpus managed by SIDBI to provide funding support to SEBI-registered Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs) that invest in startups. Indirect funding through VC/PE funds.
benefits: Access to equity capital through registered AIFs
No direct application — accessed via registered VC funds
Focus on sectors like deep tech, agri, healthcare, defence

name: BIRAC Biotechnology Ignition Grant (BIG)
description: India's flagship biotech seed grant for early-stage biotech/health-tech startups and innovators. Provides up to ₹50 lakh for proof-of-concept work over 18 months. Milestone-based disbursement.
benefits: Access to BIRAC network and mentors
Gateway to BIRAC SPARSH and PACE programmes

name: BIRAC SPARSH (Social Innovation Programme for Products)
description: Supports biotech social innovation products addressing needs of rural and underserved populations. Focus on affordable, scalable solutions for Bottom of Pyramid (BoP) markets.
benefits: Connects innovators with NGOs, CSOs, and social enterprises

name: MeitY TIDE 2.0
description: Technology Incubation and Development of Entrepreneurs 2.0 — supports ICT-enabled tech startups through 51 incubators across India. Equity-free grant support, prototype labs, and co-working.
benefits: Equity-free grant up to ₹7 lakh (pre-incubation) and ₹15 lakh (incubation)
Access to prototype labs, fab labs, and co-working space
Connect to angel networks and VCs

name: DST-NIDHI PRAYAS (Promoting & Accelerating Young and Aspiring Innovators)
description: Provides ₹10 lakh equity-free grant to individual innovators to convert ideas into prototypes within 6 months. Hosted through DST-supported incubators with proto-labs.
benefits: Access to proto-labs at host institution
Pathway to DST-NIDHI SSS for further funding

name: DST-NIDHI Seed Support System (SSS)
description: Seed support of up to ₹25 lakh to early-stage startups incubated in NIDHI-supported incubators for building market-ready product and initial business development.

name: Atal Incubation Centres (AIC) — AIM
description: World-class incubation centres set up across India to foster a culture of innovation. AICs provide incubatees with infrastructure, mentoring, seed funding access, industry linkages, and training.
benefits: Seed funding access up to ₹10 lakh via AIM

name: Atal New India Challenges (ANIC)
description: Problem-statement-based innovation challenges co-designed with Central Ministries and CPSUs. Winners receive grants up to ₹1 crore to develop, demonstrate, and deploy technology solutions at scale.

name: SIDBI SMILE (Soft Loan Fund for MSMEs)
description: Quasi-equity and term loans at below-market rates for small enterprises and startups to support expansion, technology upgradation, and working capital needs.
benefits: Loan amount ₹10 lakh to ₹25 lakh (SMILE Express) or ₹25 lakh–₹1 crore

name: Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for Micro and Small Enterprises (CGTMSE)
description: Provides collateral-free credit guarantees on loans up to ₹5 crore to micro and small enterprises through member banks and NBFCs. Reduces lender risk, enabling MSMEs to access formal credit.
benefits: 85–75% coverage of loan amount by CGTMSE
Available across 100+ member banks and NBFCs
Lower interest rates due to reduced lender risk

name: Pradhan Mantri Mudra Yojana (PMMY)
description: Provides loans up to ₹20 lakh to non-corporate, non-farm small/micro enterprises. Three categories: Shishu (up to ₹50K), Kishore (₹50K–₹5L), Tarun (₹5L–₹10L), and Tarun Plus (₹10L–₹20L).

name: Stand-Up India
description: Facilitates bank loans between ₹10 lakh and ₹1 crore to at least one SC/ST borrower and one woman borrower per bank branch for setting up a greenfield enterprise.

name: National SC-ST Hub
description: Empowers SC/ST entrepreneurs to participate in public procurement. Provides procurement and marketing support, skill development, and linkage with larger industries.
benefits: Special provisions in government procurement for SC/ST entrepreneurs
Skill development and capacity building programmes

name: NABARD Rural Innovation Fund (RIF)
description: Supports innovative projects in agriculture, micro-enterprises, and rural non-farm sectors to stimulate rural income and employment through technology adoption and market linkages.
benefits: Grant funding for rural innovation pilots

name: NABARD Agri Business Incubation Fund (ABIF)
description: Supports agri-business incubators and agri startups through funding, mentoring, and market linkages. Promotes technology and innovation in Indian agriculture.
benefits: Funding to agri startups through incubators
Market linkage with FPOs and cooperatives
funding_amount: Up to ₹25 lakh (startup grant through incubator)

name: Technology Development Board (TDB) Financial Assistance
description: Provides soft loans and equity participation for commercialising indigenously developed technology or adapting imported technology for wider domestic applications.

name: Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme
description: Incentivises domestic manufacturing by offering financial incentives of 4–20% on incremental sales from products manufactured in India over 5 years. Attracts large investments and creates jobs.

name: MSME Cluster Development Programme (CDP)
description: Provides infrastructure and common facility support for clusters of MSMEs. Focus on soft (skill development, marketing) and hard (physical infrastructure, CFC) interventions.
benefits: Common Facility Centre (CFC) with shared machinery

name: NSIC Marketing Assistance Scheme
description: Helps MSMEs participate in government tenders and domestic/international trade fairs. Provides tender document subsidy, trade fairs support, and single point registration for government procurement.
benefits: Single Point Registration for Government Tenders
Issue of Performance and Credit Rating
Subsidised participation in trade fairs and exhibitions

name: Kerala Startup Mission (KSUM) Schemes
description: Kerala Startup Mission offers the ELEVATE programme (grant up to ₹15 lakh), SPARK (for women entrepreneurs), CLIMBERS (for college students), and incubation through 14 regional incubators across Kerala.

name: Karnataka Startup Policy 2022-27
implementing_agency: Karnataka Startup Cell / Dept of IT, BT & S&T
description: Karnataka's comprehensive startup policy providing seed funding, tax incentives, procurement preference, and ecosystem support through T-Hub Bangalore, ELEVATE 100, and other programmes.

name: T-Hub Foundation — Telangana
description: World's largest startup incubator headquartered in Hyderabad. Provides physical infrastructure, mentorship, corporate partnerships, government procurement linkages, and investment facilitation.
benefits: Access to 300+ corporate and MNC partners

name: Credit Guarantee Scheme for Startups (CGSS)
description: Provides credit guarantees to startups that have been debt-funded but lack adequate collateral. Covers loans and quasi-equity instruments from banks, NBFCs, and SEBI-registered AIFs.
benefits: Guarantee cover up to 75–80% of credit facility
Enables collateral-free credit for DPIIT-recognised startups
Applicable to loans up to ₹10 crore

name: MeitY Startup Hub (MSH) — Connect & Accelerate
description: MeitY's flagship startup acceleration programme connecting tech startups with government use-cases, corporates, and investors. Runs multiple cohorts yearly across AI, FinTech, AgriTech, HealthTech themes.
benefits: Direct market access via government departments
Cloud credits from AWS, Azure, GCP

name: Women Entrepreneurship Platform (WEP)
description: National platform to foster women entrepreneurship in India. Provides mentoring, funding linkages, market access, incubation support, and compliance hand-holding.
benefits: Access to funding opportunities (grants, loans, equity)

name: Government e-Marketplace (GeM) — Startup Runway
description: GeM's Startup Runway provides DPIIT-recognised startups preferential access to government procurement worth ₹2+ lakh crore annually. Startups can list innovative products/services with relaxed eligibility.
benefits: Access to ₹2+ lakh crore government procurement
Relaxed eligibility (no prior experience requirement for startups)

name: Startup India International Market Access Programme
description: Facilitates Indian startups to expand globally through bilateral innovation bridges (with USA, UK, Israel, Japan, Singapore), soft landing programmes, and Startup20 engagement under G20.
benefits: Soft landing support in partner countries
Introductions to foreign investors and accelerators
Access to Innovation Bridges with 15+ countries

name: NSRCEL IIMB — Incubation & Acceleration
implementing_agency: N.S. Raghavan Centre for Entrepreneurial Learning (NSRCEL), IIM Bangalore
description: One of India's premier business incubators at IIM Bangalore. NSRCEL offers the Goldman Sachs 10,000 Women programme, Villgro social innovation fellowship, Women Startup Programme, and sector-specific accelerators.
benefits: Mentorship from IIM Bangalore faculty and industry leaders
Access to IIM Bangalore research, alumni (50,000+) network
Seed funding access up to ₹25 lakh
Office space at IIM Bangalore campus

name: CIIE.CO (IIM Ahmedabad) — Bharat Inclusion Initiative
implementing_agency: Centre for Innovation, Incubation and Entrepreneurship, IIM Ahmedabad
description: CIIE.CO's Bharat Inclusion Initiative focuses on inclusive tech startups solving for the masses (Bharat). Offers equity investments of $100K–$500K and deep mentorship from IIM Ahmedabad faculty.
benefits: Access to CIIE.CO network of 200+ portfolio companies
Connections to impact investors and DFIs

name: Anusandhan National Research Foundation (ANRF) Grants
description: India's apex body for funding research and development. ANRF (formerly SERB) provides grants for breakthrough research translatable to commercial applications. Startup-researcher collaboration grants available.
benefits: Grants ₹25 lakh to ₹5 crore for research projects

name: SIDBI Venture Capital — India Aspiration Fund
description: India Aspiration Fund (IAF) is a ₹2,000 crore fund-of-funds managed by SIDBI Venture Capital, investing in domestic VC/PE funds focused on MSME and startups. Drives venture capital into tier-2 and tier-3 cities.
benefits: Focus on underserved geographies and sectors

name: PM Vishwakarma Scheme
implementing_agency: Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises
description: Holistic support scheme for traditional artisans and craftspeople in 18 trades. Provides recognition via PM Vishwakarma certificate, skill training, toolkit incentive of ₹15,000, and collateral-free credit starting at ₹1 lakh (up to ₹3 lakh).
benefits: PM Vishwakarma certificate and ID card
Basic skill training of 5-7 days with ₹500/day stipend
Advanced skill training of 15 days with ₹500/day stipend
Collateral-free credit up to ₹1 lakh (Tier 1), ₹2 lakh (Tier 2)
Digital transaction incentive of ₹1 per transaction (up to 100 per month)
Marketing support via GeM, trade fairs

name: ASPIRE — A Scheme for Promotion of Innovation, Rural Industry & Entrepreneurship
description: ASPIRE promotes innovation and agro-industry in rural areas through livelihood business incubators (LBI) and technology business incubators (TBI). Provides grants to set up LBIs with resources for training and entrepreneurship.
benefits: Grants to Livelihood Business Incubators (LBIs) — up to ₹1 crore
Grants to Technology Business Incubators — up to ₹5 crore
Training and mentorship for rural entrepreneurs
Access to KVIC network for sales and distribution

name: PMEGP — Prime Minister's Employment Generation Programme
description: PMEGP provides credit-linked subsidy for self-employment and new micro-enterprises in rural and urban areas. Subsidy of 15–35% on project cost up to ₹50 lakh (manufacturing) and ₹20 lakh (services).
benefits: Subsidy 25% for urban general category (project cost up to ₹25 lakh for manufacturing)
Covers manufacturing (₹50 lakh) and services (₹20 lakh)
Bank loan arranged for remaining project cost

name: NIDHI-PRAYAS (Promoting and Accelerating Young and Aspiring Innovators)
description: DST's PRAYAS grants help innovators build prototypes and validate their technology concepts. Provides up to ₹10 lakh in early-stage prototype development funding through accredited TBIs.

name: TIDE 2.0 — Technology Incubation and Development of Entrepreneurs
description: TIDE 2.0 supports tech startups in ICT sectors (AI, IoT, Blockchain, AR/VR) through 51 host institutes. Provides seed grants of up to ₹75 lakh and equity-free support.
benefits: Seed grant up to ₹75 lakh (Phase 1: ₹25 lakh, Phase 2: ₹50 lakh)
Infrastructure and lab access at host institutes
Access to MeitY Startup Hub network

name: CGTMSE — Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for Micro and Small Enterprises
implementing_agency: CGTMSE (Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for MSE)
description: CGTMSE provides collateral-free credit guarantee to banks lending to MSEs. Covers up to 75–85% of the credit amount (up to ₹5 crore), enabling startups and MSMEs to access working capital and term loans without pledging assets.
benefits: Credit guarantee cover of 75–85% of loan amount
Wide bank coverage — all major public and private sector banks
Annual guarantee fee as low as 0.37% of credit facility
funding_amount: Guarantee for loans up to ₹5 crore

name: Atal Innovation Mission (AIM)
description: AIM promotes innovation and entrepreneurship across India through Atal Tinkering Labs (ATLs in schools), Atal Incubation Centres (AICs), and Atal New India Challenges (ANICs). Flagship government innovation initiative under NITI Aayog.
benefits: Atal Tinkering Labs with ₹20 lakh grant to schools
Atal Incubation Centres (AICs) with ₹10 crore grant
Atal Community Innovation Centres for rural areas
Atal New India Challenge for deep-tech startups (₹30 lakh–₹1 crore)
ATL Marathon — national competition for school innovators

name: Digital MSME Scheme
description: Encourages MSMEs to adopt cloud computing and ERP solutions by subsidising cloud subscription costs. Provides 50–80% subsidy on cloud services for 3 years through empanelled CSPs (Cloud Service Providers).
benefits: Subsidy of 50–80% on cloud subscription costs
Empanelled CSPs: AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, TCS, Zoho
funding_amount: Subsidy 50–80% on cloud costs (up to ₹1 lakh/year)

name: NIIF — National Investment and Infrastructure Fund (Startup Allocation)
description: NIIF is India's sovereign wealth fund that invests in infrastructure and growth companies. Allocates capital to infrastructure-linked startups and growth-stage companies at Series C and beyond.
benefits: Access to Ministry of Finance and NITI Aayog networks

name: Institution's Innovation Council (IIC) — MoE
description: IIC network enables universities and colleges to set up Innovation Councils that promote student entrepreneurship. IIC-rated institutions get recognition, grants for startup events, and direct connect to NIDHI-TBI network.
benefits: Grants for hackathons, idea festivals, and startup events
Student startup grants (up to ₹5 lakh per startup via IIC)
Access to IP filing support and mentors
Linkage to SISFS for student founders
funding_amount: Up to ₹5 lakh per student startup

name: ESIC/PF Startup Exemption (Startup India Labour Compliance)
description: DPIIT-recognised startups can self-certify compliance under 9 Labour Laws and 3 Environmental Laws, avoiding inspector raj. No inspection for first 3–5 years, reducing compliance burden significantly.
benefits: Self-certification under 9 Labour Laws (no inspector visits for 5 years)

name: RBI Regulatory Sandbox for FinTech
description: RBI's regulatory sandbox allows fintech startups to test innovative products and services under a relaxed regulatory environment with defined eligibility criteria and testing period (up to 6 months).
benefits: Test products under relaxed RBI regulations for 6 months
No enforcement action during sandbox period
Pathway to full regulatory approval on completion

name: SEBI Innovation Sandbox & Regulatory Sandbox
ministry: SEBI (Securities and Exchange Board of India)
description: SEBI's innovation and regulatory sandbox enables capital market fintechs to test products using anonymized exchange data. Helps algo trading, robo-advisory, and analytics startups validate before full SEBI registration.
benefits: Test capital market products with anonymized exchange data

name: DPIIT Fast Track Patent & IPR Support
implementing_agency: Office of Controller General of Patents, Designs & Trade Marks
description: DPIIT-recognised startups get 80% rebate on patent filing fees, fast-track examination (decisions within 30 days), and dedicated facilitators at IP offices. Trademark registration fees also 50% reduced.
benefits: Fast-track patent examination (30 days instead of 3+ years)
Free IP facilitation via CGPDTM-appointed facilitators
Free legal advice on IPR matters at DPIIT offices
funding_amount: Up to 80% rebate on IP fees
